new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PGI, actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol, in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 29 79)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.g.A., tatsächlicher Alkoholgehalt ≤ 15 % vol, in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2204 29 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI), with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol, presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2204 29 79.

coverage_explanation: The three distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 79 are: (1) still wine of fresh grapes — captured by subClassOf StillWineWithPGI2204Large which is itself subClassOf WineOfFreshGrapes; (2) protected geographical indication (PGI) — captured by the someValuesFrom restriction on ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality; (3) container volume > 2 L — captured by the someValuesFrom restriction on LargeContainer (≥ 2 L, consistent with > 2 L in trade practice); (4) actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol — captured by the decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. The note also cross-references CN 22042980 and additional note 6(a) of Chapter 22, which defines PGI — this definitional note is not further modelled here (it is a legal reference rather than a product-discriminating criterion). The container threshold in TBox is defined as ≥ 2 L (eucn:LargeContainer) whereas the CN boundary is strictly > 2 L; this minor boundary ambiguity reduces coverage slightly. Overall coverage is high but not complete due to the additional note 6(a) cross-reference and the container boundary precision.
